zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Stuart of Moidart是Muchdanach of Moidart和Mrs Muchdanach of Moidart的女兒，於1150年出生在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾莫達爾特。她於大約1162年與Dubgall Mac Somairle King of the Isles在蘇格蘭阿吉爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1207年在她的家鄉享年57歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Stuart of Moidart是Muchdanach of Moidart和Mrs Muchdanach of Moidart的女儿，于1150年出生在苏格兰音巴內-爾莫達爾特。她于大约1162年与Dubgall Mac Somairle King of the Isles在苏格兰阿吉爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1207年在她的家乡享年57岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Stuart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewart
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Stuart of Moidart was born in 1150, in Moidart, Inverness-shire, Scotland as the daughter of Muchdanach of Moidart and Mrs Muchdanach of Moidart. She married Dubgall Mac Somairle King of the Isles about 1162, in Argyll, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1207, in her hometown, at the age of 57, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Stuart of Moidart est née en 1150, à Moidart, Inverness-shire, Écosse, fille de Muchdanach of Moidart et Mrs Muchdanach of Moidart. Elle a épousé Dubgall Mac Somairle King of the Isles environ 1162, à Argyll, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1207, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Stuart of Moidart wurde 1150 in Moidart, Inverness-shire, Scotland, als Tochter von Muchdanach of Moidart und Mrs Muchdanach of Moidart geboren. Sie hat um 1162 in Argyll, Scotland Dubgall Mac Somairle King of the Isles geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1207 im Alter von 57 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Stuart of Moidart è nata il nel 1150 nel Moidart, Inverness-shire, Scotland come figlia di Muchdanach of Moidart e Mrs Muchdanach of Moidart. Lei sposato Dubgall Mac Somairle King of the Isles circa 1162, nel Argyll, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1207, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolta a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Stuart of Moidartは1150年にスコットランドインバーネス-シュアイアモイダルトでMuchdanach of MoidartとMrs Muchdanach of Moidartの娘として生まれた。約1162年にスコットランドアルグイルでDubgall Mac Somairle King of the Islesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1207年に故郷で57歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Stuart of Moidart(은)는 1150년 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르 모이더르트에서 Muchdanach of Moidart(와)과 Mrs Muchdanach of Moidart의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1162년 스컷랜드 아그이에서 Dubgall Mac Somairle King of the Isles(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1207년 그녀의 고향에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Stuart of Moidart nasceu em 1150, em Moidart, Inverness-shire, Scotland filha de Muchdanach of Moidart e Mrs Muchdanach of Moidart. Ela casou-se com Dubgall Mac Somairle King of the Isles aproximadamente 1162, em Argyll, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1207, em sua cidade natal, com 57 anos, e foi sepultada em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Stuart of Moidart родилась в 1150 г. в местности моидарт, инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия. Её родителей звали Muchdanach of Moidart и Mrs Muchdanach of Moidart. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Dubgall Mac Somairle King of the Isles около 1162 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1207 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 57 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Stuart of Moidart nació en 1150, en Moidart, Inverness-shire, Scotland como hija de Muchdanach of Moidart y Mrs Muchdanach of Moidart. Se casó con Dubgall Mac Somairle King of the Isles aproximadamente 1162, en Argyll, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1207, en su pueblo, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultada en Scotland.
